items.title: Double-tap for hands-free
items.body: Double-tap your hotkey to start persistent dictation — it keeps recording until you tap again. Perfect for long thoughts.

items.title: The human voice
items.body: The human voice is the most perfect instrument of all.

items.title: Text snippets save time
items.body: Create snippets in Vocabulary to auto-expand shortcodes. Type 'addr' and have it expand to your full address.

items.title: You're in the flow
items.body: Speaking your thoughts is one of the fastest ways to capture ideas. You're building a habit that compounds.

items.title: Drag files anywhere
items.body: Drop audio or video files onto the menu bar icon or the main window. MacParakeet handles MP3, WAV, M4A, MP4, and more.

items.title: Listening well
items.body: Most people do not listen with the intent to understand; they listen with the intent to reply.

items.title: Export in any format
items.body: Transcriptions can be exported as TXT, Markdown, SRT, VTT, DOCX, PDF, or JSON. Right-click any transcription to see options.

items.title: Your words stay yours
items.body: Everything runs on your Mac. No cloud, no accounts, no tracking. Your voice never leaves your device.

items.title: Transcribe YouTube videos
items.body: Paste a YouTube URL into the Transcribe tab. MacParakeet downloads the audio and transcribes it locally.

items.title: Words matter
items.body: One good word is worth a thousand pictures.

items.title: Teach it your words
items.body: Add custom words in Vocabulary to fix recurring misrecognitions. Great for names, jargon, and technical terms.

items.title: Faster than typing
items.body: Most people speak 3-4x faster than they type. You're choosing the most efficient input method.

items.title: Clean up your speech
items.body: Switch to Clean mode in Vocabulary to automatically remove filler words like 'um', 'uh', and 'you know'.

items.title: Speaking up
items.body: Speak your mind even if your voice shakes.

items.title: Search your history
items.body: Every dictation is saved. Use the search bar in Dictations to find past entries by content.

items.title: Building momentum
items.body: Every dictation trains your voice-first workflow. Small consistent use beats occasional marathons.
